title: Your Website Builds Repeat a Four-Step Pattern

workflow_patterns.title: Planning Every Step Before Starting
workflow_patterns.description: You ask for a website and the agent writes out a full plan before touching any files. It checks the folder, lists every piece it will make, and waits for your go-ahead.

workflow_patterns.title: Preview, Feedback, and Fix Rounds
workflow_patterns.description: The agent opens the site on your computer and shows a picture. You point out what looks wrong. It fixes the spots and shows a new picture. This repeats until the site looks right.

workflow_patterns.title: Finding Pictures That Match the Topic
workflow_patterns.description: The agent looks online for pictures that fit your subject, then drops them into the site so each card shows the right image.

workflow_patterns.title: Fixing the Broken Bits You Report
workflow_patterns.description: You tell the agent a button does not work. It reads the code, spots the missing link, patches it, and confirms the button works now.

creations.description: Builds a small website from a one-line brief, then polishes it through screenshot rounds until it looks right.
creations.skill_md_content:
---
name: website-generator
description: Builds a small website from a one-line brief, then polishes it through screenshot rounds until it looks right. Use when the user asks to build a website, create a landing page, or put together a simple site.
---

# Website Generator

## Overview

Turn a short brief ("a recipe book", "a travel log") into a working website. Plan the pieces, write them in one pass, show a screenshot, collect feedback, and fix the rough spots. Engage when the user asks for a website, landing page, or simple static site — not for editing an existing site or for server-backed apps.

## Quick Start

1. Read the brief and pick a subject (food, travel, books, etc.).
2. Lay out the files you will create.
3. Write them all.
4. Open the site on the user's computer.
5. Share a screenshot.
6. Fix what the user flags, then show a new screenshot.

## Step 1: Plan the Pieces

List what you will make before writing anything:

- one main page
- a small set of style files (one for shared values, one for the layout, one for the card look, one for the pop-up, one for motion)
- a small data file with 6 to 10 sample items
- a script that shows the items, handles search, and opens a pop-up
- a tiny icon for the browser tab

Show the plan to the user and wait for go-ahead only if the brief is unclear. Otherwise continue.

## Step 2: Build in One Pass

Write all files in a single pass. Keep every colour, spacing value, and font name in the shared values file — never in the other files. Each card should include:

- a picture
- a short title
- a one-line summary
- a tag (category)
- a pop-up view with more detail

## Step 3: Pick Pictures That Match

For each item, pick one picture that fits the subject. Use a steady source that gives the same picture every time (not a random placeholder service). A recipe card should show food, a travel card should show a place. If a picture turns out wrong, swap it — do not leave a mismatched image in place.

## Step 4: Open the Site

Start a small local server so the user can view the site in a browser. Share the web address.

## Step 5: Screenshot, Fix, Repeat

Take a full-page screenshot and show it to the user. Ask what looks off. For each note:

- read the file that controls that piece
- make a targeted edit
- take a new screenshot

Stop when the user is happy.

## Pre-Screenshot Checklist

Before sharing the first screenshot, click through the site yourself:

- every button and close-mark works
- every picture loads and matches its card
- the search box filters the cards
- the page still looks right on a phone-width window

Catching these here saves a feedback round.

## Troubleshooting

- **Wrong pictures appear**: the picture source is returning random images. Switch to a steady source and pick one picture per card by hand.
- **A button does nothing**: the button exists in the markup but no code listens to it. Add a click handler in the script file.
- **The pop-up will not close**: the close-mark is inside the pop-up but the close code is attached to the background. Move the handler to the close-mark, or close on any click outside the pop-up body.
- **Layout breaks on narrow screens**: one of the style rules has a fixed width. Switch it to a flexible width that shrinks.
creations.rationale:
The same build-preview-fix shape repeats across the session.
- One skill covers plan, build, screenshot, and fix
- Replaces four ad-hoc passes with one steady flow
